classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

title: Chapter XXIX.

source.title: ANF06. Fathers of the Third Century: Gregory Thaumaturgus, Dionysius the Great, Julius Africanus, Anatolius, and Minor Writers, Methodius, Arnobius

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Manes said: Is not that word also to the same effect which Jesus spake to the disciples, when He was demonstrating those men to be unbelieving: “Ye are of your father the devil, and the lusts of your father ye will do?”

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: By this He means, in sooth, that whatever the wicked prince of this world desired, and whatever he lusted after, he committed to writing through Moses, and by that medium gave it to men for their doing.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: For “he was a murderer from the beginning, and abode not in the truth, because there is no truth in him.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: When he speaketh a lie, he speaketh of his own: for he is a liar, and the father of it.”

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Archelaus said: Are you satisfied with what you have already adduced, or have you other statements still to make?

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Manes said: I have, indeed, many things to say, and things of greater weight even than these.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: But with these I shall content myself.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Archelaus said: By all means.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Now let us select some instance from among those statements which you allege to be on your side; so that if these be once found to have been properly dealt with, other questions may also be held to rank with them; and if the case goes otherwise, I shall come under the condemnation of the judges, that is to say, I shall have to bear the shame of defeat.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: You say, then, that the law is a ministration of death, and you admit that “death, the prince of this world, reigned from Adam even to Moses;” for the word of Scripture is this: “even over them that did not sin.”

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Manes said: Without doubt death did reign thus, for there is a duality, and these two antagonistic powers were nothing else than both unbegotten.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Archelaus said: Tell me this then,—how can an unbegotten death take a beginning at a certain time?

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: For “from Adam” is the word of Scripture, and not “before Adam.”

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Manes said: But tell me, I ask you in turn, how it obtained its kingdom over both the righteous and the sinful.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Archelaus said: When you have first admitted that it has had that kingdom from a determinate time and not from eternity, I shall tell you that.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Manes said: It is written, that “death reigned from Adam to Moses.”

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Archelaus said: And consequently it has an end, because it has had a beginning in time.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: And this saying is also true, that “death is swallowed up in victory.”

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: It is apparent, then, that death cannot be unbegotten, seeing that it is shown to have both a beginning and an end.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Manes said: But in that way it would also follow that God was its maker.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Archelaus said: By no means; away with such a supposition!

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: “For God made not death; neither hath He pleasure in the destruction of the living.”

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Manes said: God made it not; nevertheless it was made, as you admit.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Tell us, therefore, from whom it received its empire, or by whom it was created.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Archelaus said: If I give the most ample proof of the fact that death cannot have the substance of an unbegotten nature, will you not confess that there is but one God, and that an unbegotten God?

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Manes said: Continue your discourse, for your aim is to speak with subtlety.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Archelaus said: Nay, but you have put forward those allegations in such a manner, as if they were to serve you for a demonstration of an unbegotten root.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Nevertheless the positions which we have discussed above may suffice us, for by these we have shown most fully that it is impossible for the substances of two unbegotten natures to exist together.
